problems.name: Acute pulmonary embolism — segmental (low-risk)
problems.notes: Initial presentation 7/6/2027 with 6 hours of pleuritic left-sided chest pain and dyspnea. CTPA: SEGMENTAL pulmonary embolus. Troponin negative; TTE without right-heart strain — i.e., LOW-RISK PE at presentation. Essentially UNPROVOKED (no recent surgery, immobilization, long travel, or prior VTE). Started on anticoagulation with clinical improvement. (For the later progression, see the separate recurrent-PE problem.)

problems.name: Acute extensive right lower extremity deep vein thrombosis
problems.notes: Right lower extremity duplex (7/6): occlusive DVT involving the right proximal (common/proximal superficial) femoral vein, posterior tibial veins, and greater saphenous vein. Extensive clot burden. Source of the pulmonary emboli.

problems.name: Liver mass, 17 cm right lobe — indeterminate, concerning for malignancy; with right portal vein thrombus
problems.notes: Heterogeneous right-hepatic-lobe enhancement first seen incidentally on the staging-quality CTPA (radiologist favored focal fatty infiltration but included primary or metastatic malignancy in the differential). Dedicated liver MRI: a 17 cm abnormality in the posterior right lobe, additional abnormal-signal foci in the left lobe, and apparent THROMBUS within branches of the RIGHT PORTAL VEIN — radiologist with high suspicion for malignancy (primary vs metastatic) and recommends 'consider needle biopsy.' Triple-phase liver CT non-diagnostic but also concerning for malignancy. Workup ongoing; tissue diagnosis pending. (Father had colorectal cancer — metastasis is on the differential.)

problems.name: Recurrent / progressed pulmonary embolism — bilateral, intermediate-risk (submassive) with right ventricular strain
problems.diagnosedBy: Dr. Olivia Reyes (Emergency Medicine) / CTA + TTE
problems.notes: Readmission 8/10/2027. Presented to a scheduled outpatient liver biopsy with recurrent dyspnea and pleuritic chest pain after apixaban had been held 3 days per interventional radiology. CTA: PE now in BOTH main pulmonary arteries (progressed from the prior segmental embolus). NEW troponin elevation and NEW right-ventricular strain on TTE — i.e., now INTERMEDIATE-RISK (submassive) PE. Tachycardic to 115, SpO2 93% on 4 L NC. Restarted on a heparin infusion.

problems.name: Overweight
problems.notes: BMI ~29. No diabetes or hypertension. Mentioned for completeness; weight >60 kg is relevant to standard DOAC dosing.
